WSS_Clinical_Relevance: Essential for facial and limb development; decoherence explains dysmorphic features.
Linked to skeletal anomalies and mineral metabolism issues in developmental syndromes.

Biological_Function: DNA damage response and cell growth
WSS_Clinical_Relevance: High scores relate to the severe neurodevelopmental delay and microcephaly seen in WSS.
Energy production deficit contributing to Failure to Thrive and hypotonia.
Suggests a drift in systemic metabolic regulation and cardiovascular health.

Biological_Function: Lung and limb patterning transcription factor
WSS_Clinical_Relevance: Critical for developmental symmetry; disruption affects structural and skeletal integrity.
Key architect of growth; its loss of sync drives the growth retardation characteristic of WSS.

Biological_Function: Tyrosine kinase for cell adhesion and cytoskeleton
WSS_Clinical_Relevance: Involved in maintaining tissue integrity and cell-to-cell signaling during growth.

Biological_Function: Protein folding and ciliary dynein assembly
WSS_Clinical_Relevance: Relates to the pleiotropic structural and organ-level defects in chromatinopathies.
Core metabolic failure explaining systemic developmental delays and poor muscle tone.
